data.body:
PASS-1 review [T3 Integration feasibility] — PASS (overlay not-applicable, no findings).

Affirmations:
- The hermetic round-trip is an INTERNAL, already-used integration, not a new external capability: the codebase already provides the stateful fake-Jira harness (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_idempotency.py` — `_FakeJiraState`/`_FakeClient`/`_make_fake_acli_module`), an existing CI-runnable round-trip that chains the real pure differs against mock fixtures with no live Jira (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py`), inbound+outbound link sync through the production differs (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_link_sync.py`), and a verified-fake honesty contract (`tests/_jira_shape_contract.py`). The plan's "story A's fixtures/fake through the production path" maps onto this existing machinery, so the integration is achievable as described.
- The de-encode audit targets bash probes that exist and are cited precisely — the `NOT-SYNCED`/"as expected" guard is real at `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:1546-1547` (and the search across the suite confirms it is the load-bearing one). No new subcommand/endpoint is introduced.
- The one external touchpoint (live round-trip variant) is explicitly opt-in/scheduled, kept off the default suite, and reuses the established `external`-marker + `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL` + creds gating convention already documented (`tests/_jira_shape_contract.py:10-12`) and the live-probe auth preconditions in `scripts/jira-pressure-test/README.md` (JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN). No unverified external capability is depended upon.

Net: per the overlay's ANTI-FP rule (an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable), there is no asserted-but-unverified external capability here. Clean for T3.
